title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 26 - 2018: Google Earthing BouldeRoman Cloud Cities

enrichment.summary: Kyle describes a creative Google Earth project he calls the 'Kingdom of Rome,' overlaying ancient Roman-inspired villages, celebrity homes from Beverly Hills, tent cities, and mountain neighborhoods onto Boulder County's terrain. He also talks about a failed video upload the night before, his mom helping with furniture, and his girlfriend coming to visit for hiking.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Finds it fascinating and more interesting than just erasing modern infrastructure — prefers building imaginary civilizations on the terrain
Designed as a children-friendly world where conflict is replaced by bike racing and bone-healing rather than war and swords
Too time-consuming (three hours per ten minutes of content) without a second computer
Compares it to a one-day bike or horse ride range, and to what a king could see or traverse in a day

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Last night at around midnight, I tried to upload a video but it had a loud noise issue, so I had to list it as private because YouTube doesn't allow deleting videos anymore.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm creating the kingdom of Rome, which is the same exact size as Boulder County.

claims.subject: Kingdom of Rome
claims.claim: The kingdom of Rome is about the size of a one-day bike ride or a one-day horse ride, representing the range a person or animal could cover in a day.

claims.subject: Kingdom of Rome
claims.claim: A good rule of thumb for the size of a kingdom is what you can see with your eyes, like 150 miles in the distance.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I initially wanted to remove all modern buildings and roads in Boulder County to recreate ancient villages, but I found that idea boring.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I built a village on top of a mountain with 500 houses, using snapshots of houses from Boulder, including my childhood home.

claims.subject: Kingdom of Rome
claims.claim: In my imaginary Boulder Rome, people are bike racers who level up by racing, and their 'war' involves breaking bones in fights that heal after 12 hours.

claims.subject: Kingdom of Rome
claims.claim: The kingdom has a Velodrome, running tracks, and swimming pools for training, and the inhabitants are acclimated to the cold.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I created a posh neighborhood on another mountain, inspired by Beverly Hills, with houses belonging to celebrities like Denzel Washington and Eddie Murphy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I call the area above 11,500 feet 'Clown City' because it's always sunny, and I'm placing mochid castles and houses there.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to build the tallest house in the world on a mountaintop and place tents in all the good camping spots in the mountains.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to draw people doing activities like circular yoga and floating on inflatable mattresses in the lakes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm memorizing the mountain landscapes to eventually hike there, but I need to get in better shape first.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today, I helped my mom sand furniture and will repaint it tomorrow, which was a bit of exercise.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My girlfriend is visiting soon, and we plan to do hardcore hiking and record videos of the experiences.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't use green screens in my videos because the processing takes three hours and slows down my computer.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need another computer to handle video processing so I can work on other tasks simultaneously.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Last night, I collected screenshots of buildings to place in the mountains for my project.
